column1:
Raw MRI/CT data: can be matched to photographs with regular face recognition software and linking with web search

Schwarz et al. Identification of Anonymous MRI Research Participants with Face-Recognition Software. New England Journal of Medicine. 2019
Steeg et al. Re-identification of anonymised MRI head images with publicly available software: investigation of the current risk to patient privacy. eClinicalMedicine. 2024
column3:
Face removed MRI/CT data. Depends on whether ears and teeth are present - reverse reconstruction can be done 

Abramian D, Eklund A. Refacing: Reconstructing Anonymized Facial Features Using GANS. ISBI 2019 
Xiao Y, et al. Refacing Defaced MRI with PixelCNN. IJCNN 2022
column5:
Skull stripped MRI: cross-linkage between MRI databases can retrieve individuals based on their brain pattern

Puglisi et al. DeepBrainPrint: A Novel Contrastive Framework for Brain MRI Re-Identification. PMLR 2024 
Guillon et al. Identification of rare cortical folding patterns using unsupervised deep learning. Imaging Neuroscience. 2024
column7:
Tissue maps (scalp, skull, GM, WM, CSF), VBM, SDM: can distinguish among individuals

Takao e la (2015). Brain morphology is individual-specific information. Magnetic Resonance Imaging. 33
column8: Tissue maps (scalp, skull, GM, WM, CSF), VBM, SDM
column9:
Morphometric maps (thickness, curvature, etc):   it is unique, and the re-identification rate is very high

Duan et al. Cortical Foldingprint for infant identification. Proc IEEE Int Symp Biomed Imaging. 2019 
Wachinger et al. BrainPrint: a discriminative characterization of brain morphology. Neuroimage. 2015
column11:
Morphometric models (e.g. cortical mesh, Boundary Element Models for EEG biophysical models, etc ): depends if the face is included
Sareen (2021). Exploring MEG brain fingerprints: Evaluation, pitfalls, and interpretations. NeuroImage. 2021
column12: Morphometric models (e.g. cortical mesh, Boundary Element Models for EEG biophysical models, etc )
column13:
Synthetic structural MRI and CT for the same imaging type (e.g. learn from T1w make T1w). A generative model can memorize images and thus reproduce them
Usman et al. Beware of diffusion models for synthesizing medical images—a comparison with GANs in terms of memorizing brain MRI and chest x-ray images. Mach Learn: Sci Technol. 2025 
Dar  et al. Unconditional latent diffusion models memorize patient imaging data. Nat Biomed Eng. 2025
column14: Synthetic structural MRI and CT - same image type
column15:
Synthetic structural MRI and CT for different imaging type (.e.g learn from MRI, make CT, learn from T1w make FLAIR T2w)
- currently no direct evidence -
column16: Synthetic structural MRI and CT for different imaging type
column17:
Table of morphometric (volume, thickness, etc) or qMRI (T1 time) information (ROI based). Brains have unique patterns, such as one can discriminate individuals from patterns in ROI
Valizadeh et al. Identification of individual subjects on the basis of their brain anatomical features. Sci Rep. 2018
column18: Table of morphometric (volume, thickness, etc) or qMRI (T1 time) information (ROI based)
column19:
Raw fMRI data: the face may be visible for high resolution data, but not been successfully demonstrated when tested
Schwarz et al (2023) A face-off of MRI research sequences by their need for de-facing. NeuroImage 276
column23:
Perfusion (CE) and Flow (TOF) imaging
- no evidence -
column27:
Pre-process dynamic data (fMRI, ASL) in subject space
- no evidence -
column28: Pre-process dynamic data (fMRI, ASL) in subject space
column29:
Spatially normalized dynamic data (fMRI, ASL): Brain images retain their main distinguishing information and features
Chen et al (2013) Confirming the Diversity of the Brain after Normalization: An Approach Based on Identity Authentication. PLOS ONE, 8
column30:
Space-Time decompositions from dynamic imaging (fMRI, ASL, fNIRS, PET), for instance ICA maps and associated time courses: ability to distinguish individuals
Wang et al (2017) A Novel Feature-Map Based ICA Model for Identifying the Individual, Intra/Inter-Group Brain Networks across Multiple fMRI Datasets. Front Neurosci. 2017

Xie et al.(2018) Whole-brain connectivity dynamics reflect both task-specific and individual-specific modulation: A multitask study. Neuroimage. 180
column31: Space-Time decompositions from dynamic tomographic imaging (fMRI, fNIRS, PET), for instance ICA maps and associated time courses
column32:
Task-based activation maps (% signal change, t-values, etc)
- no evidence -

Derived DWI data (FA, MD, track profiles): some tracks  may have unique characteristics

Raw PET images: depending on the scanner and tracer, faces can be reconstructed

PET parametric (e.g. SUV) and kinetic parameter maps (e.g.KI, BPnd)

column42:
Raw and Preprocessed electrophysiological data (EEG, MEG): contains unique features (also in the non-brain signals such as eye blinks, muscle activity), allowing discriminate among subjects
Meng et al. 2024. Protecting Multiple Types of Privacy Simultaneously in EEG-based Brain-Computer Interfaces IEEE 20832088
Da Silva Castanheira et al (2021). Brief segments of neurophysiological activity enable individual differentiation. Nat Commun. 12

column43: Raw and Preprocessed electrophysiological data (EEG, MEG)

intra cranial EEG: Within subject same-session identification accuracy  is above 90%, and persist across sessions
Belay & Katsigiannis (2025) Human Intracranial EEG Biometric Identification. 47th Annual International Conference of the IEEE Engineering in Medicine and Biology Society

Power spectra (EEG, iEEG, MEG): highly subject specific

Thomas & Vinod (2017) Toward EEG-Based Biometric Systems: The Great Potential of Brain-Wave-Based Biometrics. IEEE Systems, Man, and Cybernetics Magazine. 3

Event related electrophysiological response (ERP-ERF): ERPs are highly reliable within subjects but moderately stable over time

Raw or preprocessed fNIRS data: dHB time courses over many sensors are unique to discriminate participants

DWI connectivity matrices: connectome can distinguish among individuals with high performence

MEEG connectivity matrices: can distinguish among individuals
